reasoning: Taylor Swift's 'The Life of a Showgirl' debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 with 4 million units and has held the spot for 5 consecutive weeks. However, to reach 18 straight weeks, it would have to stay at #1 through late February 2026. Given the precipitous drop in its weekly units (falling to 120,000 by week 5) and the inevitable dominance of legacy holiday albums (such as Michael Buble's 'Christmas') in late December, an 18-week uninterrupted reign is effectively impossible in the modern streaming era. The Polymarket price in late October was 1%, confirming this assessment. I am submitting the minimum allowed probability bound of 5%.
